narration: You just paid off your car loan. Congrats — except your credit score just dropped forty points. Here's why the system is built against you.
visual: Dark background. Credit score gauge animating from 720 down to 680. Red down arrow. Badge: DOWN 40 PTS.

narration: Your credit score has five parts. Account age makes up fifteen percent — and closing an old account can tank that number overnight.
visual: Light background. Animated pie chart of 5 credit score components. The Account Age slice (15%) is enlarged and highlighted in amber.

narration: Credit mix is another ten percent. Lenders want to see cards, loans, and a mortgage all at once. Pay one off, and you lose that bonus.
visual: Dark background. Three SVG icons side by side: credit card, car (auto loan), house (mortgage). Badge: PAY ONE OFF = LOSE THE MIX.

narration: Wild right? The algorithm rewards a small balance. Carrying one to nine percent utilization actually scores higher than zero percent.
visual: Light background. SVG credit card. Utilization bar animating to 9%. Label: 1-9% UTILIZATION SWEET SPOT FOR YOUR SCORE.

narration: Here's the fifty-dollar-a-month trick: keep old accounts open with a tiny recurring charge, like a streaming sub. Account age stays alive.
visual: Dark background. Credit card with streaming play-button icon. $50/MONTH label. Two green checkmarks: ACCOUNT STAYS OPEN and ACCOUNT AGE PRESERVED.

narration: The credit score system wasn't built to reward you for getting out of debt. It was built to keep you in it. Follow for more money traps nobody teaches.
visual: Light background. Person silhouette trapped behind red vertical bars. CTA badge: FOLLOW FOR MORE MONEY TRAPS.
